author: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon
book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.

metadata.author: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon

title: Canon XXX.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: It is sacrilege to degrade a bishop to the rank of a presbyter; but, if they are for just cause removed from episcopal functions, neither ought they to have the position of a Presbyter; and if they have been displaced without any charge, they shall be restored to their episcopal dignity.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And Anatolius, the most reverend Archbishop of Constantinople, said: If those who are alleged to have been removed from the episcopal dignity to the order of presbyter, have indeed been condemned for any sufficient causes, clearly they are not worthy of the honour of a presbyter.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But if they have been forced down into the lower rank without just cause, they are worthy, if they appear guiltless, to receive again both the dignity and priesthood of the Episcopate.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And all the most reverend Bishops cried out:

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The judgment of the Fathers is right.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: We all say the same.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The Fathers have righteously decided.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Let the sentence of the Archbishops prevail.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And the most magnificent and glorious judges said:

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Let the pleasure of the Holy Synod be established for all time.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Canon XXIX.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: He is sacrilegious who degrades a bishop to the rank of a presbyter.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: For he that is guilty of crime is unworthy of the priesthood.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But he that was deposed without cause, let him be [still] bishop.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: What precedes and follows the so-called canon is abbreviated from the IVth Session of the Council (L. and C., Conc., Tom.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: I have followed a usual Greek method of printing it.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: This so-called canon is nothing but a verbal copy of a passage from the minutes of the fourth session in the matter of Photius of Tyre and Eustathius of Berytus.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Moreover, it does not possess the peculiar form which we find in all the genuine canons of Chalcedon, and in almost all ecclesiastical canons in general; on the contrary, there adheres to it a portion of the debate, of which it is a fragment, in which Anatolius is introduced as speaking.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Besides it is wanting in all the old Greek, as well as in the Latin collections of canons, and in those of John of Antioch and of Photius, and has only been appended to the twenty-eight genuine canons of Chalcedon from the fact that a later transcriber thought fit to add to the genuine canons the general and important principle contained in the place in question of the fourth session.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Accordingly, this so-called canon is certainly an ecclesiastical rule declared at Chalcedon, and in so far a κανών, but it was not added as a canon proper to the other twenty-eight by the Synod.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: From the Fourth Session of the same Holy Synod, having reference to the matter of the Egyptian Bishops.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The most magnificent and glorious judges, and the whole Senate, said:
